steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is B, $\frac{7qt}{q-4t}$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Isolate s terms on one side
steps.content:
Starting with $21qrt - 3qrs + 12rst = 0$, move $21qrt$ to the right:
$$-3qrs + 12rst = -21qrt$$

steps.title: Factor s and divide
steps.content:
Factor $s$ from the left side:
$$s(-3qr + 12rt) = -21qrt$$
Divide both sides by $(-3qr + 12rt)$:
$$s = \frac{-21qrt}{-3qr + 12rt}$$

steps.title: Simplify by factoring the denominator
steps.content:
Factor $-3r(q - 4t)$ from the denominator:
$$s = \frac{-21qrt}{-3r(q - 4t)} = \frac{7qt}{q - 4t}$$
That confirms B is correct.
